mitigations.description: Bridge expires at immutable horizon timestamp (52 weeks from deployment)
mitigations.label: Expiry

mitigations.description: Happy-path economic bound: mint amount mirrors tokens legitimately burned on the remote chain by the BurnMintTokenPool's lockOrBurn (i.e. the remote user burned the same amount before bridging). As long as each remote TokenPool and its OnRamp are honest, no net ZCHF is created — supply is only moved across chains.
mitigations.label: Remote Burn Bound

mitigations.description: Per-chain inbound CCIP rate limit (enforced on-chain): the BurnMintTokenPool runs a token-bucket limiter BEFORE calling mint (_consumeInboundRateLimit in releaseOrMint). Max burst per chain ≈ 500k ZCHF on Base/Gnosis, 100–200k on others; refills LINEARLY at ~10k/hour (Base) or ~5k/hour (others). Aggregate burst ≈ 1.7M ZCHF. This cap holds even if the CCIP Committing DON and RMN are both compromised — they cannot bypass the pool's own rate limiter. Rate limits are admin-changeable by the FPS governance.
mitigations.label: CCIP Rate Limit

mitigations.description: Trust assumption (NOT an on-chain cap): underneath the rate limit, message authenticity relies on Chainlink CCIP. The Committing DON signs the source-chain Merkle root, RMN / ARMProxy independently blesses it, then the OffRamp delivers it. If Committing DON AND RMN collude, forged mint messages could be delivered, but they are still bounded by the on-chain rate limiter above, which is the true cryptographic cap.
mitigations.label: CCIP DON+RMN Trust

mitigations.description: Flash loan only — mints ZCHF to itself and burns the exact same amount via burnFrom in the same transaction
mitigations.label: Flash Loan

mitigations.description: Mints accrued interest to Savings contract; bounded by currentRatePPM × elapsed time × account balance
Mints accrued interest to Savings contract; bounded by currentRatePPM × elapsed time × account balance

Happy-path economic bound: the reported loss amount is bounded by actual losses on positions in the remote chain's BridgedFrankencoin. Each remote BridgedFrankencoin restricts its own coverLoss() to registered minters (e.g. remote MintingHub / position contracts), so the loss value in the CCIP payload mirrors real collateral-backed debt defaults on that chain. As long as each remote bridge's minter set and code are honest, this function cannot be used to claim arbitrary losses.
mitigations.label: Remote Minter Bound

mitigations.description: If the Committing DON AND RMN collude (or are both compromised), a forged loss message with an arbitrary amount could drain the reserve up to its full balance. Emergency mitigation is RMN curse, a governance-layer response. Trust assumption (NOT an on-chain cap): message authenticity relies on Chainlink CCIP. The data path is validated by (1) the Committing DON signing the source-chain Merkle root, (2) RMN / ARMProxy independently blessing it, then (3) the OffRamp delivering it.
mitigations.label: CCIP DON+RMN Trust

mitigations.description: Only transfers BridgeAccounting's own ZCHF balance into the reserve (additive accounting). Cannot drain or reduce reserve funds regardless of the CCIP message contents — the `amount` argument is not the payload value, it's `ZCHF.balanceOf(address(this))`. Even under a full CCIP compromise (Committing DON + RMN colluding) a forged profit message cannot cause a drain: the worst an attacker can do is force BridgeAccounting to forward its existing balance, which ends up in the reserve (benefiting FPS holders).
mitigations.label: Additive Only

mitigations.description: Caller must sacrifice their own votes proportionally — budget deducted from caller first, then applied to targets up to that budget
Only callable when system equity < MINIMUM_EQUITY (1000 ZCHF) — system must be near-insolvent
Caller must be qualified FPS holder with 2% quorum of total votes (via checkQualified with helpers)
riskPremium bounded to 100%
reservePPM must be between CHALLENGER_REWARD (2%) and 100%
Minimum collateral value must be at least 5000 ZCHF worth
Requires 1000 ZCHF irrevocable opening fee and collateral deposit from caller
Collateral token must revert on failed transfers (verified at position creation)
Challenger must deposit matching collateral amount; position must be registered (validPos)
Bidder must pay ZCHF at Dutch auction price; market mechanism with declining price over challenge period
Must pay expiredPurchasePrice which starts at 10x liquidation price and declines linearly over two challenge periods
7-day mandatory delay before proposed rate takes effect
Caller must be qualified FPS holder with 2% quorum of total votes (via equity.checkQualified)
Rate bounded by uint24 type — maximum ~1677% APR (16,777,215 PPM)
7-day mandatory delay before proposed rate takes effect
Caller must be qualified FPS holder with 2% quorum of total votes (via equity.checkQualified)
Rate bounded by uint24 type — maximum ~1677% APR (16,777,215 PPM)
